root.flag.file: Path to the activity log file (or data directory for timewarrior)
start.flag.json: Output the created activity in JSON format
stop.flag.json: Output the stopped activity in JSON format
add.flag.json: Output the created activity in JSON format
note.short: Append a note to an existing activity
note.long:
Append a note to an existing activity after it was logged.

If no date-index is provided, appends to the last activity.
To update a specific activity, provide its key (YYYY-MM-DD-NN).

Examples:
  tock note "Followed up with summary"
  tock note 2026-03-14-02 "Added meeting outcome"
  tock note 2026-03-14-02 "Added meeting outcome" --json
note.flag.json: Output the updated activity in JSON format
tag.short: Append tags to an existing activity
tag.long:
Append tags to an existing activity after it was logged.

If no date-index is provided, appends tags to the last activity.
To update a specific activity, provide its key (YYYY-MM-DD-NN).

Examples:
  tock tag review urgent
  tock tag 2026-03-14-02 review urgent
  tock tag 2026-03-14-02 review urgent --json
tag.flag.json: Output the updated activity in JSON format
tag.error.required: at least one tag is required
current.long:
Lists all currently running activities.

You can format the output using Go templates with the --format flag.
Available variables:
  .Project      - Project name
  .Description  - Activity description
  .StartTime    - Start time (time.Time object)
  .EndTime      - End time (time.Time object, usually nil for running activities)
  .Duration     - Activity duration (time.Duration object)
  .DurationHMS  - Duration formatted as HH:MM:SS
current.flag.format: Format output using a Go template (e.g. '{{.Project}}: {{.Duration}}'). See --help for variables.
continue.flag.description: the description of the new activity
continue.flag.project: the project to which the new activity belongs
continue.flag.time: the time for changing the activity status (HH:MM)
continue.flag.json: Output the created activity in JSON format
last.flag.number: Number of recent activities to show
report.long: Generate a report of tracked activities aggregated by project
report.flag.project: Filter by project and aggregate by description
report.empty: No activities found for the specified period.
export.long: Export report output as txt, csv, or json
export.flag.stdout: Print output to stdout instead of writing a file
remove.long:
Remove an activity from the log.

If no argument is provided, removes the last activity.
To remove a specific activity, provide its index ID (YYYY-MM-DD-NN).

Examples:
  tock remove                     # Remove last activity
  tock remove -y                  # Remove last activity without confirmation
  tock remove 2023-10-15-01       # Remove specific activity
remove.flag.json: Output the removed activity in JSON format
remove.confirm.title: About to remove:
watch.flag.stop: Stop the activity when exiting watch mode
calendar.help:
Use arrows to navigate:
 - 'j'/'k' to scroll details
 - 'n'/'p' for next/prev month
 - 'q' to quit
list.help: Press 'q' to quit, left/right to change date
analyze.long: Generate a scientific analysis of your work habits, including deep work score, context switching, and chronotype estimation.
analyze.title: 🧠 Productivity Analysis
ical.long:
Generate iCal (.ics) file(s). Provide a key (YYYY-MM-DD-NN) for a single task, a date (YYYY-MM-DD) with --path to export all tasks for that day, or no arguments to export all tasks.
Use --open to automatically import into the system calendar (macOS only).
ical.opened.all: Opened combined calendar events in macOS Calendar
ical.opened.single: Opened calendar event in macOS Calendar
ical.empty_date: No activities found for this date.
ical.error.path_required: output directory (--path) is required for bulk export unless --open is used
ical.error.activity_not_found: activity not found (index %d out of range 1-%d)
